message:
feat(core): add prepared table-engine allocation reuse metrics

Introduce two new hot-path profile counters to track whether prepared
statement execution reuses cached table-engine instances or allocates
fresh ones:

- FSQLITE_PREPARED_TABLE_ENGINE_FRESH_ALLOCS: incremented when a
  prepared statement creates a new TableEngine for a cursor slot that
  had no prior engine or whose schema epoch has drifted.

- FSQLITE_PREPARED_TABLE_ENGINE_REUSES: incremented when an existing
  TableEngine is reused because its schema epoch still matches.

These counters feed into HotPathProfileSnapshot and are wired through
reset_hot_path_profile / hot_path_profile_snapshot / the Display impl
for profile formatting.

Also expand the connection's prepared-statement execution to cache and
reuse TableEngine instances across repeated executions of the same
prepared statement, keyed by cursor index and schema epoch. This avoids
redundant btree root-page lookups and schema validation on hot query
replay paths.

Update perf_runner test snapshot to include the two new counters plus
the previously-missing memory_autocommit_fast_path_begins field.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2741,7 +2741,10 @@ mod tests {
             statement_dispatch_background_gates: 1,
             prepared_schema_refreshes: 1,
             pager_publication_refreshes: 1,
+            memory_autocommit_fast_path_begins: 1,
             column_default_evaluation_passes: 2,
+            prepared_table_engine_fresh_allocs: 1,
+            prepared_table_engine_reuses: 1,
             record_decode: RecordHotPathProfileSnapshot {
                 parse_record_calls: 4,
                 parse_record_into_calls: 2,
